page: The Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC processor. It features 64 vCPUs, which correspond to 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz under a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 with VNNI and BF16 support, AES cryptography acceleration, and secure virtualization via SVM.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory efficiency. The single-core stress-ng performance ranks in the top tier, while multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores fall within the top 25% of tested hardware. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% and uncached large-block read bandwidth measured at 340.62 GB/sec. For data processing, the server achieves strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and database operations, alongside a top-tier text generation speed of 53.85 tokens/sec using a medium 7B LLM model.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective alternative for highly parallelized workloads that do not require dedicated core guarantees. The combination of 64 vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and fast NVMe storage makes it well-suited for high-throughput web serving, database hosting, redis caching, and medium-scale machine learning inference. However, the shared CPU model represents a performance tradeoff under sustained, maximum-load compute tasks compared to dedicated instances.
description: The Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 64 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC processor on the x86_64 architecture. It includes 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and operates on a shared CPU allocation model. The hardware supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and AES. In benchmark testing, the server delivers top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside strong multi-core processing and database throughput. This configuration is highly suitable for parallelized workloads such as database operations, static web serving, redis caching, and medium-scale LLM text generation. It offers a resource-dense, cost-effective solution for multi-threaded applications, though the shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, guaranteed compute performance.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 64 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density AMD EPYC server featuring sixty-four vCPUs, fast NVMe storage, and top-tier memory performance for parallelized data workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC processor with 64 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM
960 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
AVX-512 and cryptography hardware acceleration support
Shared CPU allocation model optimized for cost-effective scaling
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and LLM inference
